raw_consumer_statement: I filed a credit card dispute with Citibank for a charge on my XXXX from a 3rd party travel vendor XXXX for a hotel stay at the XXXX XXXX on XXXX & XX/XX/year>. The hotel reservation was made & paid for through XXXX for a total of {$580.00} with a transaction date of XX/XX/year>. On the 2nd night of our stay, we had to leave early due to a noisy guest in the next room. The XXXX XXXX manager agreed to give us a refund of 1 night of our stay which amounts to XXXX ( that email thread has been attached ). The refund request was sent to XXXX which handles reservations for XXXX ( also attached ) as I was told the refund needed to go through the 3rd party travel vendor. I never got the refund from XXXX for the 1 night of the stay of XXXX after repeated follow up attempts so I filed a credit card dispute with Citibank for the XXXX noted above on XX/XX/year> Citibank Case ID # XXXX. Citibank responded by letter requesting a " credit slip '' which I do not have as the issue is that I never got the credit from XXXX applied to my Citibank credit card account that I was pursuing. After I explained this to Citibank in a follow up email, they sent me a letter stating my dispute was closed and I would owe the full amount as I didn't provide the credit slip ( doc also attached ). Again, there is no credit slip which is the entire point of my credit card dispute. I have attached the refund request sent by the XXXX XXXX to XXXX XXXX I called Citbank back after my case was closed & explained this to a customer service representative who advised they would reopen my dispute. I tried to speak to the Citibank transaction dispute department but I was told that is not possible. Citibank again requested the credit slip in a follow up letter so I responded by email again advising there is no credit slip which is the exactly the reason I am disputing the charge. Citibank sent me a follow up letter on XX/XX/year> advising my dispute was closed again and I would owe the full amount. Their reason was the same as previously listed which was that I did not provide a credit slip. My impressions are that Citibank 's standard procedure is to side with the merchant as their response letters appear to be form letters and do not match the specifics of my charge dispute. Citibank 's online reputation is that they always favor the merchant in these type of disputes and their handling of my case appear to confirm this as their reasons for closing my case do not match my specific dispute. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.
